response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced generalist with a genuine developer-tooling bent — he built C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments, authored test runners and assertion libraries, and works on developer/agent tooling at Stripe. However, the role's core requirements are largely unmet: his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with only incidental Java, no visible Kotlin work, no Spring or enterprise-framework experience, and no demonstrated JVM depth. The IDE-internals interest is real but the JVM/enterprise-Java foundation this position demands is missing.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong developer-tooling track record (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Proven work on complex, high-scale systems (low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only minimal, dated Java exposure
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise JVM framework background
Primary depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the JVM ecosystem
